title: T-002 — Implement POST /api/v1/onboarding/step API endpoint
prompt:
Implement the onboarding step progression endpoint.

PRD §14.3 requires a stateful wizard that:
- Accepts step number and form data (depends on step)
- Updates organizations.onboarding_state: current_step, completed_steps, started_at, last_activity_at
- Validates input (enum steps 1-6, required fields per step)
- Returns step success + next_step_hint or error
- Supports resume: step N → can jump to step N (if already completed) or next unvisited step

Steps 4 and 5 are skippable (skip button):
- Step 4 (First Product) redirects to 03-TECHNICAL create product
- Step 5 (First WO) redirects to 04-PLANNING-BASIC create WO
Sending skip=true marks the step as skipped (skipped_steps array) and moves to next.

Acceptance:
- POST /api/v1/onboarding/step accepts {"step_number": 1-6, "data": {...}, "skip": false}
- Updates onboarding_state atomically
- Validates step transitions (no jump from 1 to 5)
- Skippable steps (4, 5) accept skip=true
- Returns JSON: {"status": "success", "current_step": N, "next_step": N+1 or null}
- On error returns 400/422 with reason

Test: RED test verifies state transitions (step 1→2→3→skip 4→5→6), started_at is set once, last_activity_at updates.

pipeline_inputs.description: API endpoint to progress through onboarding steps with state persistence
pipeline_inputs.details: Create POST /api/v1/onboarding/step endpoint (FastAPI). Input: {"step_number": 1-6, "data": object, "skip": bool}. Logic: validate step is valid integer 1-6; if skip=true and step in [4,5], add to skipped_steps and move to next; otherwise validate step form data (step 1: org name/timezone/locale/currency; step 2: warehouse name/type/code; step 3: location zone/bin; steps 4,5: skip only; step 6: no data); update organizations.onboarding_state atomically with current_step, completed_steps (append step if not already completed), started_at (set on first call), last_activity_at (always update); return {"status": "success", "current_step": N, "next_step": N+1 or null if completed}. Reject jumps (e.g. 1→5 unless all 2-4 completed). Enforce step 6 can only be reached after steps 1-5 (completed or skipped).
acceptance_criteria: Given new org at step 1, when POST /api/v1/onboarding/step {"step_number": 1, "data": {...}}, then current_step becomes 2 and completed_steps includes 1
Given org at step 4, when POST with skip=true, then step 4 added to skipped_steps and current_step becomes 5
Given org at step 6, when all prior steps completed or skipped, then next_step is null and status is 'complete'
Given org at step 2, when attempt to jump to step 5, then return 422 with 'skipped steps must be 4 or 5' reason
test_strategy: Write RED test: test_onboarding_step_progression() verifies 1→2→3→skip4→5→6 state chain
Write RED test: test_onboarding_step_invalid_jump() verifies 1→5 is rejected
Verify endpoint response shape matches prompt (status, current_step, next_step keys)
risk_red_lines: Do not allow step progression outside the 1-6 range
Do not allow backward movement (current_step > new step_number)
Do not skip non-optional steps 1, 2, 3, or 6
Do not double-add a step to completed_steps if called twice
